game.matchup: San Antonio Spurs at Oklahoma City Thunder

phase_analysis.first_half: SAS led 51-44. Wembanyama already had 14/10 and Spurs held a 29-20 rebounding edge. OKC's primary issue was SGA suppression: 1-5 FG, 4 points before halftime.
phase_analysis.third_quarter: Both teams scored 29. OKC briefly reclaimed momentum, but SAS stabilized behind Wembanyama free throw pressure and Harper disruption.
phase_analysis.fourth_quarter: OKC won Q4 28-21, forcing 101-101 regulation. SGA and Caruso generated the comeback, but SAS survived with Wembanyama interior gravity and late-clock execution.
phase_analysis.first_overtime: Both teams scored 7. OKC led by 3 with under a minute, but Wembanyama hit the tying 28-foot three with 27 seconds left.
phase_analysis.second_overtime: SAS dominated 14-7. Wembanyama produced 9 points and 4 rebounds in the period; OKC shot 2-9 and scored zero paint points.

model_diagnostics.variance_notes: Regulation finished 101-101, meaning OKC TT Over 106.5 required OT and hit because of extended-game tail risk. SAS No Over 108.5 lost primarily from OT extension and Wembanyama takeover. Castle 20+ missed because his offensive role shifted toward facilitation and high turnover pressure, finishing with 17 points and 11 assists.

forward_prep.next_series_focus: CLE at NYK Eastern Conference Finals prep
forward_prep.notes: Only three-game regular season H2H sample; require broader matchup model using playoff form, lineup health, possession profile, rebounding/boxout layer, and halfcourt efficiency rather than H2H-only weighting.
